title: festuca rubra subsp. kirelowii
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. kirelowii, also known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its vibrant green foliage and reddish-brown inflorescences. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. kirelowii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
